title: Leontodon obliquus
intro: Leontodon obliquus, also known as Hawkbit, is a perennial plant species belonging to the dandelion family (Asteraceae). Traditionally, Leontodon obliquus has been used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including inflammation, fever, and respiratory problems, due to its potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Leontodon obliquus include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Leontodon obliquus is commonly used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to alleviate digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and boost the immune system.
taxonomy: Leontodon obliquus is a species of flowering plant in the dandelion tribe within the sunflower family (Asteraceae). It belongs to the genus Leontodon, which includes several species of dandelion-like plants. The genus Leontodon is a member of the tribe Cichorieae, which also includes other plants such as sow thistles and hawk's beard. Further classification places the Cichorieae tribe within the subfamily Cichorioideae, a part of the large sunflower family Asteraceae. This classification reflects the evolutionary relationships between these plant species and their shared characteristics.
